option: Light-dominant: warm or cool white base with dark ink text
avoid_when: subject is nightlife/gaming/dev-tool where dark carries meaning

option: Dark-dominant: layered near-black surfaces (3+ elevation steps not flat black)
Paper: warm off-white with e-ink restraint and print-like rules
Split/duotone: two large color fields dividing the page into zones
Mid-tone base: colored (not white not black) canvas e.g. deep olive/clay/slate
Warm: reds/ochres/browns/cream family
Cool: blues/greens/slate family
Neutral + one temperature spike: gray-led with a single hot or cold accent zone
avoid_when: when the whole page needs emotional warmth

option: Muted throughout: dusty desaturated palette with tonal depth
One saturated hero color used structurally (backgrounds/zones) not just links
High-key clash: 2-3 saturated colors deliberately colliding
Sharp: radius 0-2px with weight carried by borders and rules
Soft: radius 8-16px consistent scale
Pill/rounded-full on interactive + sharp on containers (mixed intentional)
Irregular: hand-drawn borders or asymmetric organic masks
Flat solid fields with no decoration — precision spacing carries the design
avoid_when: when it slides into flat emptiness (see two-bar check)

option: Subtle grain/noise overlay on large color fields
Paper/print artifacts: registration marks/stamps/perforation motifs
Layered depth: overlapping elements with insets, badges, real shadows
3D or dimensional renders as consistent illustration system
Airy: generous negative space around fewer confident elements
avoid_when: content marketing pages needing depth signals for SEO

option: Considered-dense: magazine density with strict grid discipline
Rhythmic: alternating airy hero moments with dense proof sections
Asymmetric split: 40/60 or 30/70 vertical division with contrasting content types
Editorial columns: multi-column article grid with pull elements crossing columns
Sidebar-anchored: fixed sidebar identity/navigation with scrolling canvas
Diagonal flow: sections offset alternately left/right creating a zigzag reading path
Overlap stack: sections whose elements bleed into neighboring sections
Horizontal scroll chapter inside vertical page (one, deliberate, keyboard-accessible)
avoid_when: when a11y budget can't cover proper keyboard support

option: Broken grid: items placed off-grid with intentional misalignment
Color-block zoning: sections defined by full-bleed background color shifts not whitespace
Sticky-scroll narrative: pinned element that transforms while copy scrolls past
avoid_when: short pages; CWV budget tight (INP risk)

option: Serif authority: characterful display serif + neutral grotesk body
Grotesk contrast: extreme weight/width contrast within one grotesk family
Mono-technical: monospace for labels/data/nav + humanist body
Display-loud: hero type at 12-20vw as the primary visual element
Condensed-impact: tall condensed display + wide-tracking caption system
Script accent: one script/handwritten face used only for 2-3 word accents
Static: zero animation — composition alone carries the page
Restrained: one signature easing curve, 2-3 orchestrated moments max
Orchestrated entrance: one page-load sequence, then calm
Interactive-expressive: cursor-following, hover physics, canvas effects
Oversized typographic hero with deliberate line breaks doubling as navigation or image mask
Custom cursor or cursor-following element on brand moments only
Subject-derived motif system: an icon/shape from the subject's real world repeated as structure (coffee ring, blueprint line, vinyl groove, stitch pattern)
avoid_when: only when motif is genuinely derived not decorative

option: Interactive canvas moment: one WebGL/canvas element tied to the subject
Live/real element: actual data, real-time state, genuine artifact embedded (live availability, real reviews, actual menu)
avoid_when: when no real data exists — never fake it

option: Image treatment system: one consistent unusual treatment (duotone, halftone, cut-out collage, irregular masks) across ALL imagery
Structural annotation: hand-drawn or technical annotations (arrows, margin notes, dimension lines) as a real wayfinding layer
Physical-world artifact skeuomorph: one element styled as a real object from the sector (ticket, receipt, luggage tag, prescription pad, polaroid)
Editorial photography full-bleed with consistent grade
avoid_when: no-budget projects without good source imagery

option: Duotone/tritone processed photography matching palette exactly
Illustration system: one consistent style, custom-feeling, never mixed
Type-only: no imagery, typography and color carry everything
Cut-out/collage: subjects isolated from backgrounds, layered with shapes
Abstract generative/geometric art derived from brand geometry
